text.title: Ruby on Rails Service Objects Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real Rails service-object rollouts.

antipattern.symptom: OrderService grows to 600 LoC with #create, #update, #cancel, #refund, #ship methods.
antipattern.fix: Split into PlaceOrderService + UpdateOrderService + CancelOrderService + RefundOrderService + ShipOrderService — one action per class.

antipattern.symptom: Callers wrap service in rescue blocks; success/failure paths invisible until try/catch.
antipattern.root-cause: Service raises for business failure (insufficient stock).
antipattern.fix: Return ServiceResult.failure(error: :insufficient_stock); callers check result.success? without rescue.

antipattern.symptom: Service tests need full Rails boot; suite slow; tests fail when HTTP stack changes.
antipattern.root-cause: Service called from request spec only; no isolated unit test.
antipattern.fix: Write spec/services/place_order_service_spec.rb without `rails_helper` (use `spec_helper`); pass collaborators as mocks.

antipattern.symptom: `Order.find(1).touch` triggers PlaceOrderService.call which sends email; reproduction in console is destructive.
antipattern.fix: Remove callback; have controller/job explicitly call PlaceOrderService.call.
